verdict: REFINES the law (honest, predicted partly wrong): the flu network is strongly DIRECTED (DY 78%; the symmetrization null confirms directedness drives the edge, +10->-15 pts), but -- contrary to our pre-registered expectation -- its net-transmitter COINCIDES with its loudest state (Texas). So the loudest-vs-transmitter GAP that produces a large anticipatory advantage is ABSENT, and transmitter-targeting only modestly beats the loudest-node heuristic (x1.1). The operative quantity is the loudest-transmitter gap, not directedness alone; flu (coincident transmitter) and the 2008 equity crash (symmetric factor) are two distinct ways that gap is small, both giving ARRO ~ greedy as the law requires.
